mechanism-commit-innovation.description: Commit to a concrete mechanism change and validate it with fair and stress-tested experiments.
guardrails: Name the exact component being added/rewired and why it fixes the defect.
Include fair baselines and at least one failure-mode stress test.

counterfactual-contrast.description: Add counterfactual or rare-regime data components and inject them into train/eval flow.
guardrails: Limit newly added data channels to avoid uncontrolled complexity.
Show where the counterfactual channel is injected into training and evaluation.

adaptive-constraint-hybridization.description: Hybridize constraints/penalties with the objective while controlling side effects.
guardrails: Keep the added constraint measurable and tied to a single objective term.
Use gating to disable the new constraint under unfavorable budget or data regimes.

surgical-modularity.description: Touch only one weak block via replace/rewire and prove improvement with focused ablation.
guardrails: Include a mandatory ablation isolating the touched block.

data-contract-repair.description: Repair supervision/data contract first; only add modules when contract repair alone is insufficient.
guardrails: Prioritize protocol fixes before architecture changes.

multi-scale-coordinator.description: Add a coordinator for cross-scale routing/aggregation and control overhead with gating.
guardrails: Specify routing conflict resolution and added latency constraints.
Gate coordinator usage for budget-sensitive scenarios.

self-supervised-corrector.description: Add a self-supervised corrector to learn residuals/invariants and inject corrections safely.
guardrails: Define correction injection points and anti-overcorrection controls.
Use stress tests to expose residual failure pockets.

theory-transfer-injection.description: Inject a theory-backed mechanism from another domain as a first-class component.
guardrails: State the transferred mechanism and where it is wired into the system.
Use gating and stress tests to ensure added theory module is controllable.

evaluation-contract-overhaul.description: Reshape evaluation/training contracts first, then optionally adjust components based on contract failures.
guardrails: Ensure protocol changes directly expose previously hidden failure modes.
Keep model changes secondary to evaluation contract repair.
